title: agoseris laciniata
intro: Agoseris laciniata, also known as the cut-leaved agoseris or laciniated agoseris, is a perennial wildflower native to North America, characterized by its distinctive cut or lobed leaves. Traditionally, this plant has been used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe wounds and reduce swelling. The health benefits of Agoseris laciniata include its potential to aid in the treatment of skin conditions, such as eczema and acne, as well as its ability to support digestive health. Agoseris laciniata can be prepared for medicinal use through various herbal preparations, including tinctures, infusions, and salves, which can be applied topically to the affected area.
